title: FP Estimator dataset metadata.json
description: Schema for data/<app-name>/metadata.json. This documents the shape server/handler/data.go and app/index.html actually consume, including which fields are truly required vs which have a safe fallback when omitted, null, or empty. It is a documentation/IDE-validation aid (reference it via a "$schema" key in your metadata.json for editor autocomplete/validation) — it is NOT enforced at runtime; the server and app are deliberately tolerant of missing optional fields (see docs/adr/0007-json-schema-for-dataset-validation.md).

$schema.description: Point this at ../schema/metadata.schema.json (relative to your data/<app-name>/ folder) for IDE validation.

version.description: Optional. Shown in the footer as 'vX.Y.Z'; falls back to '1.0.0' if omitted.

lastUpdated.description: Optional, informational only — not currently rendered.

description.description: Optional. Not rendered directly; projectSummary.description is what's shown on the Project tab.

projectSummary.description: Optional. Omit entirely to show a generic empty-state on the Project tab instead of the hero/overview cards.

logo.description: Optional Iconify icon name (e.g. 'mdi:rocket-launch'). Falls back to a generic rocket icon.

logoColor.description: Optional CSS color for the logo icon. Falls back to white.

name.description: Optional. Falls back to metadata.brandPrefix, then 'Project'.

tagline.description: Optional. Falls back to an empty string (hero renders with no subtitle).

description.description: Optional. Falls back to 'No description available.'

techStack.description: Optional. Omit, null, or [] all correctly hide the Technology Stack block (no empty header shown).

industry.description: Optional. Industry line is hidden entirely if omitted.

targetUsers.description: Optional. Omit, null, or [] correctly hide the Target Users block.

valuePropositions.description: Optional. Omit, null, or [] correctly hide the Value Propositions block.

links.description: Optional. Only recognized keys render a button; unknown keys are ignored.

keyJourneys.description: Optional. Omit, null, or [] correctly hide the 'Core User Journeys' card entirely. Each entry's own 'steps' is independently optional — a journey with no steps renders 'No steps defined for this journey.' instead of breaking the page.

name.description: Required per-journey (falls back to 'Untitled journey' if somehow absent).

icon.description: Optional Iconify icon name. Falls back to 'mdi:walk'.

steps.description: Optional. Omit, null, or [] renders a 'No steps defined' placeholder for that one journey card — it no longer crashes the whole app (fixed 2026-07-22; previously an absent/null 'steps' threw and blanked every section, not just this card).

projectConfig.description: Required. This is the one object the server treats as authoritative for branding/FP defaults/product list.

brandPrefix.description: Optional but strongly recommended — drives the page title and the Excel/PDF export filename prefix. Falls back to 'FP'. Note the app footer is deliberately product-branded and does NOT use this.

defaultHourlyRate.description: Optional. Falls back to 75. Seeds the Rate/hr input, which the user can then override live.

defaultPDR.description: Optional. NOMINAL hours per Function Point — the human baseline BEFORE any AI adjustment (conventionally ~8 for human-driven enterprise work). Falls back to 8. Must NOT be pre-discounted for AI: the app composes effective PDR = defaultPDR x product(active productivityFactors), so an AI discount baked in here would double-count against those factors. Express AI speedup in productivityFactors instead. Seeds a live input in Effort & Cost.

currency.description: Optional. Falls back to 'USD'. Not currently rendered as a symbol — informational.

products.description: Required, and must be non-empty for a meaningful dataset (an empty list renders 'No products defined').

shortCode.description: Optional. Shown under the product name on the Project tab; falls back to the product JSON's own 'id' field, then nothing.

description.description: Optional. Not currently surfaced separately from the product JSON's own content.

dataFile.description: Required — filename (relative to this dataset's folder) of the product JSON to merge in. A missing/unreadable file is silently skipped (matches the prior combine-wbs.js behavior) — the product just won't appear, with no error surfaced to the UI. Double-check this if a product goes missing.

fpConfig.description: Optional in full — omit and the server defaults every field below.

fpWeights.description: Optional. Falls back to {} (all capabilities compute 0 FP if this is empty — the totals will just be 0, not an error).

gscDefinitions.description: Optional. If omitted, null, or [], the app falls back to the standard IFPUG 14-factor list (fixed 2026-07-22 — previously this dataset field was defined but silently ignored; the app always used its own hardcoded 14, discarding any dataset-authored defaults).

default.description: Optional GSC default rating (0-5). Falls back to 0 if absent.

vafBase.description: Optional. Base term of the Value Adjustment Factor formula: VAF = vafBase + (vafIncrement x TDI). Falls back to the IFPUG standard 0.65. Changing it means results are no longer IFPUG-conformant. Exposed as a live input under Effort & Cost > Advanced.

vafIncrement.description: Optional. Per-TDI-point term of the VAF formula. Falls back to the IFPUG standard 0.01, which bounds adjustment to +/-35% across the 14 GSC factors.

techStackFactors.description: Optional. Map of {key: {label, icon}}. DOCUMENTATION ONLY — populates the Technology Stack selector and the Tech Stack tab, and is intended to be carried into generated proposals/SOWs. It does NOT affect hours or cost by design; effort is driven by nominal PDR and productivityFactors alone. (A `factor` field used to exist here and was removed 2026-07-22 — it never fed the math and was misleading.)

productivityFactors.description: Optional. Map of {key: {label, factor, description}}. Rendered as checkboxes in Effort & Cost; EVERY checked entry's `factor` multiplies into effective PDR (multi-select, they compound). Values below 1 reduce effort, above 1 increase it. None are active by default — the user opts in. These are where the AI speedup belongs: keep `defaultPDR` at the human baseline and express agent-led gains here, so the discount has one source of truth and stays separately calibratable. Use vendor-neutral labels ('AI Agent-Assisted', 'AI Agent-Led'), not product names. (Before 2026-07-22 these were displayed but never applied.)

sdlcPhases.description: Optional. If omitted or null, the app falls back to its own built-in SDLC phase breakdown (a hardcoded default list in app/index.html) rather than erroring.

statusDefinitions.description: Optional. Keys are status ids used on capabilities (e.g. 'completed', 'partial', 'in-progress', 'roadmap', 'beta'). If omitted entirely, the status legend falls back to generic English labels ('Completed', 'Partial', etc.) for every key.

label.description: Drives the status-legend filter text. This is the field that lets a dataset relabel e.g. 'roadmap' as 'Build New'.

glossary.description: Optional — array or object, rendered as-is on the Glossary tab. Omit to show that tab empty.
